agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID propagated correctly via X-Trace-ID header; structured logs correlate."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID included in Kafka message headers; publish side is correct."},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker does NOT extract trace ID from Kafka message headers. Generates a new trace ID, severing the distributed trace. All downstream correlation from this point is lost."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"NotificationService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without any trace ID header. NotificationService starts a fresh trace with no link to the originating order request."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"AuditService","status":"risk","issue":"No trace ID propagated via HTTP. AuditService cannot correlate audit records back to the originating request. OrderService has the trace ID in-process but fails to send it."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"AuditService","status":"broken","issue":"No trace ID propagated, and FulfillmentWorker already has a broken trace ID (newly generated), so even if it were sent it would not correlate to the original request."}],"contract_compliance":"Trace propagation contract is honored only between API Gateway and OrderService (including Kafka publish). The contract is violated at every boundary downstream of Kafka consumption. There is no shared specification or enforcement mechanism for trace propagation across service boundaries.","data_flow_integrity":"An order placed via the API Gateway can only be traced through two of five services. Once the event crosses the Kafka boundary into FulfillmentWorker, the request becomes three independent, uncorrelated traces. A single customer order generates at least three disjoint trace trees, making end-to-end debugging impossible."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker logs use a locally-generated trace ID that cannot be joined to upstream logs — any grep/search by the original trace ID will miss fulfillment, notification, and audit activity","NotificationService logs are completely orphaned from the originating order","AuditService logs cannot be correlated to either the order request or the fulfillment flow","No evidence of a shared structured logging schema (e.g., consistent field names for trace_id, span_id, order_id) across services"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Per-service metrics exist (counters, histograms) but lack cross-service and business-level visibility","gaps":["No cross-service SLO tracking (e.g., end-to-end order-to-fulfillment latency)","No Kafka consumer lag metrics mentioned for FulfillmentWorker — cannot detect processing backlog","No error-rate correlation across service boundaries (e.g., spike in FulfillmentWorker errors vs. OrderService publish rate)","No business metrics: orders created vs. orders fulfilled vs. notifications sent — cannot detect silent drops","No metric for trace-ID-missing or trace-ID-mismatch to detect propagation failures programmatically"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Partial — only API Gateway → OrderService → Kafka publish. Approximately 40% of the request path is traced; 60% is dark.","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker severs the distributed trace by not extracting the incoming trace ID from Kafka headers","NotificationService is completely untraced relative to the originating request","AuditService is completely untraced relative to the originating request","No parent-child span linking across the Kafka async boundary","No trace sampling strategy or trace completeness monitoring mentioned"]},"debugging_capability":"Severely limited. At 3am, an on-call engineer investigating a failed order notification would need to: (1) find the order's trace ID from API Gateway/OrderService logs, (2) manually search FulfillmentWorker logs by timestamp and payload content (not trace ID) to find the corresponding processing, (3) repeat the same timestamp-based guesswork for NotificationService and AuditService. In a high-throughput system this is effectively impossible without adding order_id as a secondary correlation key and even then requires manual stitching across three log stores."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"FulfillmentWorker crashes mid-processing after generating new trace ID","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Without the original trace ID, dead-letter queue messages cannot be correlated to the originating order trace. Extract and preserve the original trace ID so DLQ triage can link back to the source request."},{"scenario":"Kafka consumer lag spike causes delayed fulfillment","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add consumer lag metrics (records-lag-max per partition) and alert when lag exceeds SLO threshold. Without this, delayed orders are invisible until customers complain."},{"scenario":"NotificationService is down or slow","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Duration histograms exist but without trace propagation there is no way to attribute notification failures to specific orders. Add circuit breaker on FulfillmentWorker → NotificationService call and propagate trace ID for correlation."},{"scenario":"AuditService receives duplicate or out-of-order calls from OrderService and FulfillmentWorker","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Without a shared trace/order ID, AuditService cannot deduplicate or order audit events correctly. Propagate trace ID and include a sequence/causality indicator."},{"scenario":"Silent message drop between Kafka publish and FulfillmentWorker consumption","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a reconciliation metric: orders published vs. orders consumed (by order ID). Alert on divergence. Currently this gap is invisible."}],"retry_config":"Not specified for any service. Kafka consumer retry/backoff policy and HTTP retry policies for FulfillmentWorker→NotificationService and *→AuditService calls are unknown.","timeout_config":"Not specified. HTTP call timeouts between FulfillmentWorker→NotificationService and *→AuditService are unknown. Risk of cascading slowdowns.","circuit_breakers":"No evidence of circuit breakers on any inter-service HTTP call.","graceful_degradation":"No evidence of graceful degradation strategies. If NotificationService is down, it is unclear whether FulfillmentWorker fails the entire fulfillment or proceeds without notification."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"Cannot assess — no deployment strategy information provided. However, the broken tracing means that even if rollback is possible, diagnosing WHEN to rollback is severely impaired because cross-service failure correlation does not exist.","monitoring_coverage":"Per-service metrics only. No end-to-end order lifecycle monitoring. No SLO tracking. No alerting on trace propagation failures. Estimated coverage: individual service health is visible, but system-level health is a blind spot.","on_call_documentation":"Insufficient. An on-call engineer cannot follow a request end-to-end. Runbooks would need to document the manual timestamp-correlation workaround, which is error-prone and slow under incident pressure.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["FulfillmentWorker Kafka consumer config must extract trace ID from message headers","All HTTP clients must inject X-Trace-ID header from current trace context","Consider OpenTelemetry collector or equivalent for unified trace aggregation"],"infra_provisioning":["Distributed tracing backend (Jaeger/Tempo/X-Ray) if not already present to visualize cross-service traces","Cross-service SLO dashboard and alerting rules"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database changes identified in this review."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Distributed trace is severed at FulfillmentWorker Kafka consumer","reason":"60% of the order processing path (FulfillmentWorker, NotificationService, AuditService) cannot be correlated to the originating request. This is the single highest-impact observability gap: it makes end-to-end debugging, SLO measurement, and incident response effectively impossible for any issue that crosses the Kafka boundary.","routed_to":"Coach K — FulfillmentWorker team must extract and propagate trace ID from Kafka message headers before this system is production-ready"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No cross-service SLO tracking or business-level metrics","reason":"Individual service counters and histograms cannot answer 'what percentage of orders are fulfilled within X minutes' or 'are notifications being sent for all fulfilled orders'. Silent failures across service boundaries will go undetected.","routed_to":"Coach K — team needs end-to-end SLO definition and reconciliation metrics before production launch"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"AuditService receives calls from two services with no trace context","reason":"AuditService is a shared dependency called by both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker, neither of which propagates trace ID. Audit records cannot be correlated to source requests, undermining the fundamental purpose of an audit trail. This is both an observability gap and a correctness risk.","routed_to":"Coach K — requires contract change: all callers of AuditService must include X-Trace-ID and order_id in requests"}],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Trace propagation break at Kafka boundary is definitive based on the stated behavior","Integration assessment of all six service-to-service interactions","Impact on debugging and incident response capability"],"low_confidence_areas":["Resilience mechanisms (retries, timeouts, circuit breakers) — no information provided, assessed as absent","Deployment and rollback capability — no information provided","Whether services use a shared tracing library that could make the fix straightforward"],"assumptions":["Services do not have an alternative correlation mechanism (e.g., order_id propagation) that partially compensates for broken trace IDs","No OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation is in place that might capture some spans despite missing explicit propagation","Kafka is used with at-least-once delivery semantics"]}}

Generates a new trac

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this order processing system's observability and produce my structured JSON output.

Key issues:
1. Trace ID breaks at FulfillmentWorker (Kafka consumer doesn't extract trace ID)
2. NotificationService gets no trace ID
3. AuditService gets no trace ID from either caller
4. No cross-service SLO tracking
5. Effectively, tracing is only correlated for API Gateway → OrderService

Let me produce the assessment.
